finding: As of ABC's 6 July 2026 report, SA Police had not yet presented its findings to the coroner, while Abbott said he expected a report soon.
effect_on_hypotheses: Weakens any claim that the Webb identity is officially closed; supports an intelligence requirement for coroner/SAPOL confirmation.
outstanding_questions: Official SAPOL/coroner report not yet obtained.

finding: The 2021 exhumation was an official SAPOL/FSSA action, part of Operation Persevere and linked to DNA recovery attempts.
effect_on_hypotheses: Supports DNA/exhumation development; neutral on all death-mechanism hypotheses.
outstanding_questions: Original SAPOL page should be archived if reachable.

finding: Abbott announced in July 2022 that the Somerton Man was Carl Charles Webb, based on DNA from hairs in the plaster bust and genealogical matching.
effect_on_hypotheses: Strongly supports Webb identity as a research claim; weakens generic unknown-foreigner/spy identity claims, but does not close official identity.
outstanding_questions: Needs official coroner/SAPOL determination and lab documentation.

finding: Astrea Forensics states it extracted, sequenced and genotyped DNA from a single 5 cm rootless hair strand.
effect_on_hypotheses: Supports feasibility of the hair-based DNA result; neutral on cause of death.
outstanding_questions: No public chain-of-custody/lab report or peer-reviewed article was located.

finding: Fitzpatrick described the genealogy method as matching DNA data to relatives through GEDmatch and family-tree reconstruction; she reported a Keane-line match and maternal-side confirmation.
effect_on_hypotheses: Supports Webb identification pathway; supports investigation of Keane clothing link.
outstanding_questions: Full match list, kits, consent, and bioinformatics report unavailable.

finding: ABC reports civil-record details for Carl Webb: born 16 Nov 1905 in Footscray, married Dorothy Jean Robertson on 4 Oct 1941, instrument maker.
effect_on_hypotheses: Supports Webb biographical reconstruction; neutral on cause/manner.

finding: A 5 Oct 1951 Trove legal notice states Dorothy Jean Webb instituted divorce proceedings against Carl Webb, formerly of Bromby Street, South Yarra, then parts unknown.
effect_on_hypotheses: Supports Webb disappearance/desertion timeline if Webb is the deceased; supports personal-context inquiries.
confidence: High for notice; Moderate for linkage
outstanding_questions: Need full divorce file and direct civil documents.

finding: ABC Australian Story reported family photographs from the 1920s said to show Charles Webb, supplied by Webb family members; it also stated the finding was not yet verified by SAPOL.
effect_on_hypotheses: Supports facial/biographical reconstruction; neutral on death mechanism; reinforces official-status gap.
outstanding_questions: Need image rights, original album provenance and comparative method.

finding: The John Keane name on clothing may be explainable through Webb family links to nephew John Russell Keane, but this remains inferential.
effect_on_hypotheses: Supports ordinary-family clothing transfer alternative; weakens label/name-as-spy-clue readings.
outstanding_questions: Need original garments, handwriting/label analysis and John Keane property records.

finding: Contemporary reporting in February 1949 said fingerprints were sent to all Australian and many foreign police headquarters and were not on file.
effect_on_hypotheses: Supports long-running unidentified status; neutral on cause/manner; weakens claim that non-identification alone proves espionage.

finding: Contemporary July 1949 reporting says the Rubaiyat book was handed to Det.-Sgt. Leane by an Adelaide businessman, had the last-page words torn out, and contained phone numbers plus capital letters not deciphered.
effect_on_hypotheses: Supports Rubaiyat/code evidence; neutral toward spy unless operational linkage appears.
outstanding_questions: Need original police report, book photos, paper/print test results.

finding: The Boxall/Jestyn Rubaiyat lead was publicly reported as having broken down after police located Alfred Boxall and his book.
effect_on_hypotheses: Weakens a direct Boxall-as-deceased lead; neutral on broader Thomson/Jestyn relevance.
outstanding_questions: Need police interview record and Boxall service file cross-reference.

finding: State Records SA says it holds the open 1949 inquest file but not police files, because the case remained open; the later inquest file was restricted at the time of the guide.
effect_on_hypotheses: Supports archive acquisition strategy and explains missing police-primary materials.
outstanding_questions: Need current access status check for 1958 file and police files.

finding: Contemporary inquest reporting says the coroner treated murder as not ruled out, death as probably poison and identity as unknown.
effect_on_hypotheses: Supports conservative handling of homicide/poisoning possibilities; does not prove either.
outstanding_questions: Prefer DOC-0001 transcript for final wording.

finding: The Webb identification, if officially confirmed, would materially weaken broad foreign-agent identity theories, but would not by itself determine cause or manner of death.
effect_on_hypotheses: Weakens espionage identity framing; neutral on poisoning/suicide/homicide without more records.

finding: No peer-reviewed public DNA identification paper or official coroner finding was located in this OSINT pass.
effect_on_hypotheses: Weakens overconfident wording; supports intelligence gap IR-identity.
outstanding_questions: Repeat search in academic databases and request official records.

finding: No reliable primary isotope-analysis report was located in this OSINT pass; isotope claims remain leads only.
outstanding_questions: Locate original Abbott/ACAD hair isotope documentation if extant.

finding: Smithsonian's 2025 update treats the Webb identity as believed/identified by researchers while also noting the official confirmation gap.
effect_on_hypotheses: Supports public-narrative update; does not add primary proof.
outstanding_questions: Use only as synthesis, not core evidence.
